description: strip dangling optional depends_on entries from a resolved compose file (resolved.yml) so docker compose up -d does not abort on an undefined-service reference left by profile filtering
rules: edits a COPY of resolved.yml under record_store; never mutates the source artifact or the checked-in compose files
drops only dict-form entries explicitly marked required: false whose target service is absent
a dangling REQUIRED dep — or any dangling list-form (short-syntax) dep — is reported and the run exits non-zero rather than silently dropped, since dropping it would mask a broken deployment
MUST run after `docker compose ... config > resolved.yml` and before `docker compose -f resolved.yml up -d`
this is the same normalization step the deploy perk performs inline (Step 3d)
usage: Set RESOLVED (path to a resolved.yml produced by `docker compose --env-file <env> config`). Output: normalize.json (audit) + a normalized resolved.yml copy under record_store.
limitation: Operates on an already-resolved compose artifact only; it does not run `docker compose config`. Requires python3 with pyyaml. Refuses to normalize when a dangling dependency is required.
